anchor_fixtures._provenance_note: Operating table centroid extracted from architect Rev-E theatre layout dated 2026-05-22; central position with surgical luminaire pendant; bed top at 1000mm AFF per equipment schedule.

zones._derivation_note: Group 2 patient envelope per §710: 1.5m radius cylinder around the patient position, 12-sided regular polygon approximation centred at (3000,2500). Height_min=0, height_max=2500mm covers the surgical working volume. RCD-protection NOT applied inside the envelope — Group 2 mandates a medical IT (insulated) system instead per §710. Sockets and switches in the envelope MUST be medical-isolating-socket type connected to the IT system.

electrical_constraints._clause_citation: BS 7671:2018 §710 + BS EN 61557-8 (IMD 8 s alarm response) + HTM 06-01 (NHS precedence)
electrical_constraints._derivation_note: Medical IT system required for the Group 2 patient envelope per §710 (no Part 7 sub-clauses valid; §710 top-level only). IMD per BS EN 61557-8 with 8 s alarm response. Isolating transformer at 5 kVA — within the 3.15-8 kVA plausibility band per D-4 (no flag raised). Supplementary bonding ≤0.2 Ω per §710. HTM 06-01 NHS technical memorandum takes precedence in NHS sites and defines safety_service_category 1 (no-break).

Supplementary equipotential bonding within the Group 2 patient environment per §710 + §701.415.2. Resistance ≤0.2 Ω (measured 0.15 Ω per input override). Bonding extends to operating table frame, anaesthesia machine ground, scrub sink, and any other metallic touchable surface in the envelope.

assumptions: Group 2 patient envelope approximated as 12-sided regular polygon (1.5m radius cylinder) per the geometry-validation convention recorded in spec §5.7.
Isolating transformer sized at 5 kVA per input medical_it_system_override; within the 3.15-8 kVA plausibility band so no D-4 flag is raised.
Safety service category 1 (no-break) sourced from HTM 06-01 NHS technical memorandum, NOT from BS 7671 §710 (no such sub-clause exists in the verified file).

invariants.evidence: 1 anchor (patient_1) produces 1 zone (medical_envelope_group_2). No overlaps with sibling zones (single zone).
existing_fixtures_audit[] has 2 compliant entries; non_compliance_flags[] empty. One-to-one trivially holds.

medical_envelope_group_2 zone present; sibling medical_it_system constraint present with applies_to_zone_ids=[zone_patient_1_envelope_g2] AND imd_alarm_response_time_s_max=8 AND supplementary_bonding_max_resistance_ohm=0.2 per BS 7671:2018 §710 + BS EN 61557-8. Also satisfies the schema-level allOf if/then enforcing this combination.
room_type=medical_group_2_theatre, not bathroom/shower_room/sauna. §701.411.3.3 + §703.411.3.3 RCD blanket N/A — INV-04 vacuously passes per spec §7.2.
Not a pool hall. §702.415.1 main bonding N/A.

2 fixtures audited: surg_lum_1 (surgical luminaire IPx2, 230V, NON-RCD because IT-system circuit) at (3000,2500,3000) — ceiling-mounted at z=3000 > Zone height_max=2500 → outside envelope; surgical luminaires are medical-equipment-of-trade per HTM 06-01 with IT system feed bypassing RCD; PASS. iso_socket_1 (medical_isolating_socket IPx4, 230V, NON-RCD) at (4500,2500,1100) inside the envelope — type medical_isolating_socket explicitly permitted (not in prohibited_fixture_types); the no-RCD is correct because the IT system replaces RCD per §710; PASS.
compliant=true ∧ violation_count_critical=0 ∧ non_compliance_flags=[] ∧ no silent overlaps. Self-consistency holds.

rationale.chat_summary: Standalone Group 2 operating theatre — 6000×5000mm with single patient envelope (1.5m radius cylinder, 12-sided polygon). FULL medical_it_system constraint per §710 + BS EN 61557-8 (IMD 8s alarm) + HTM 06-01 (5kVA transformer, safety category 1, 0.2Ω bonding). supplementary_equipotential_bonding to patient-environment metalwork. 2 fixtures both compliant. INV-03 PASSES. D-4 not raised because 5kVA sits inside 3.15-8kVA plausibility band.

sections.title: Standards applied
sections.summary: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §710 governs medical electrical services; only the top-level §710 citation is valid in the verified file (no §710 sub-clauses are verified-file-valid per the spec §3.2 banned-list). BS EN 61557-8 defines IMD with 8 s alarm response time. HTM 06-01 is the NHS technical memorandum where safety_service_category 1/2/3 actually lives (NOT BS 7671). BS EN 60601 governs medical-electrical-equipment safety.

sections.title: Zone derivation logic
sections.summary: Group 2 patient envelope = 1.5m radius cylinder centred on the patient position, height 0 to 2500mm AFF. The schema requires polygons (cannot store a cylinder primitive directly), so the runtime emits a 12-sided regular polygon approximation per spec §5.7 geometry-validation convention. Vertices computed at 30° intervals from (3000,2500) with r=1500.

sections.title: Honest disclosures
sections.summary: Three assumptions: 12-sided polygon approximation per spec §5.7; 5 kVA isolating transformer inside 3.15-8 kVA plausibility band so D-4 not flagged; safety_service_category 1 sourced from HTM 06-01, NOT from BS 7671 §710. The 8 s IMD alarm-response figure is the BS EN 61557-8 constant (NOT 0.5 s, which was a prior misattribution corrected during the brainstorm). 0.2 Ω supplementary bonding resistance is the §710 maximum.

sections.title: Failure modes considered
sections.summary: If imd_alarm_response_time_s_max were set to anything other than 8, the schema-level const enforcement would FAIL — INV-03 would FAIL. If supplementary_bonding_max_resistance_ohm were != 0.2, schema enforcement would FAIL. If isolating_transformer_va_min were <3150 or >8000, D-4 would fire (5000 sits inside the band so no flag). If a generic 230V socket were placed inside the envelope (instead of a medical_isolating_socket), INV-08 sub-rule type_prohibited would FAIL.
